pattern: Track the smallest sum you cannot yet form: while it is at most the target, either consume the next existing number if it fits or patch in exactly that smallest missing value, which at most doubles your reachable range each patch.
hints: Keep a running value equal to one more than the largest contiguous sum you can currently build from one upward.
If the next available number is no larger than that boundary, absorbing it extends your reach by its value at no cost.
Otherwise patch in the boundary value itself, which doubles the reach; count that patch and repeat until the boundary exceeds the target.

edge_inputs.description: one patch fills the gap at two
two patches needed for the wider range
already covers the range so zero patches
empty-of-ones array forces patching from one
all numbers too large, must patch from scratch
single one covers a small target via doubling
large existing numbers far past the boundary are useless early

stress.description: sorted random array with a moderate target, kept small enough for a reachability oracle
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    k = rng.randint(1, 40)
    nums = sorted(rng.randint(1, 60) for _ in range(k))
    n = rng.randint(1, min(size, 20000))
    return [nums, n]
stress.note: target capped so the set-based oracle stays fast while the greedy is exercised
